envReport 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Produce a map of occurrence records

Source: R/occ_data_map.R
occ_data_map.Rd

Map points from a data frame with coordinates and display them binned by decade. Also includes option to bin points spatially to speed up drawing of large datasets. Alternative to data_name_map.

occ_data_map(
  df,
  x = "long",
  y = "lat",
  year = "year",
  aoi = NULL,
  in_crs = 4326,
  out_crs = 4326,
  min_year = 1950,
  title_prefix = NULL,
  bin = FALSE,
  bin_method = NULL,
  bin_size = NULL,
  aoi_args = list(),
  aoi_name = NULL
)

Arguments

df

Dataframe with (at least) coordinate and year columns.

x

Character name of column with X coordinates (e.g. "long").

y

Character name of column with Y coordinates (e.g. "lat").

year

Character name of column with year.

aoi

Either a sf::bbox object or a polygon that sets the map extent. If null, calculated as the bounding box of records.

in_crs

Coordinate reference system of x and y in df. Can be given as any coordinate reference system recognised by sf::st_crs (e.g. EPSG code).

out_crs

Coordinate reference system of resulting map.

min_year

Minimum year for dots breaks. Useful for datasets with long temporal tails eg gbif that otherwise results in very long legends.

title_prefix

Name to add to records legend title i.e "title_prefix records" .

bin

Logical to bin by x/y/year. Recommended for large datasets to reduce data overlap, write time, and memory use.

bin_method

How to bin locations, if bin==TRUE. One of "round" (to round coords; faster but less granular) or "cell" (to bin by raster cells; slower but more specific).

bin_size

Integer. Either number of decimal places to round coords to, or cell resolution (in m).

aoi_args

Named list of additional aesthetics to send to tmap::tm_polygons. Default is black border with linewidth=2.

aoi_name

Character. Name of aoi (if using) to add to legend

Value

tmap object. Additional layers or map parameters can be added using occ_data_map(df) + tmap::tm_blah()

Contents

Developed by Nigel Willoughby, Simeon Zylinski.
